title: tetraberlinia korupensis
intro: Tetraberlinia korupensis, also known as the Korup tree, is a plant species native to the tropical rainforests of West Africa. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The health benefits of Tetraberlinia korupensis include the treatment of fever, wounds, and various gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of malaria. Herbal preparations of Tetraberlinia korupensis include extracts, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to create medicinal teas, ointments, and other treatments.
